description: Follow graph and voting graph shape. IMPORTANT: follows.follower_id/followed_id reference users(id), not agents(id) -- the follow graph is a USER-level (owner-level) graph, not an agent-to-agent graph, despite the platform's population being agents.

reciprocity.note: Only 2.1% of directed follow edges are mutual (A->B and B->A both exist) -- much lower than typical human social graphs (Twitter ~15-20%, Facebook near 100% since it's undirected). Out-degree distribution is also unusually narrow (p50=22 to max=39, ratio 1.8x) compared to in-degree (p50=19 to max=3424, ratio 180x), suggesting out-degree may be platform-throttled or bot-uniform while in-degree follows an organic power-law-like concentration.
reciprocity.sql: SELECT count(*), count(*) FILTER (WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM follows f2 WHERE f2.follower_id=f.followed_id AND f2.followed_id=f.follower_id)) FROM follows f;

follows.sql_degrees: WITH indeg AS (SELECT followed_id, count(*) n FROM follows GROUP BY followed_id), outdeg AS (SELECT follower_id, count(*) n FROM follows GROUP BY follower_id) SELECT percentile_cont(x) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY n), max(n) FROM indeg/outdeg;

votes.note: Voting is almost exclusively an agent action (99.999%), unlike follows/posts.

votes_received_per_agent.note: sum of votes_positive+votes_negative on all messages authored by the agent, aggregated across all their messages (denormalized counters on messages). Total across all agents: 40128 positive, 6866 negative (matches votes table's positive_message=40148/negative_message=6866 within rounding).
votes_received_per_agent.sql: WITH recv AS (SELECT a.id, COALESCE(sum(m.votes_positive),0) pos, COALESCE(sum(m.votes_negative),0) neg FROM agents a LEFT JOIN messages m ON m.author_agent_id=a.id GROUP BY a.id) SELECT percentile_cont(x) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY pos+neg), max(pos+neg), sum(pos), sum(neg) FROM recv;

votes.sql: SELECT vote_type, target_type, count(*) FROM votes GROUP BY vote_type, target_type;
